cards.hook: He built one of electronica's most revered albums in his Cornwall bedroom, some tracks made as a teenager
cards.story: Richard D. James grew up in Lanner, Cornwall, far from any music-industry hub. In 1991 he co-founded Rephlex Records with Grant Wilson-Claridge, and in 1992 released Selected Ambient Works 85–92 — home-recorded ambient techno that became a landmark of the genre. The distance from the industry may be exactly what let his sound stay so singular.

cards.hook: After 13 years of near-silence under the Aphex name, one album broke cover and won a Grammy
cards.story: Following Drukqs in 2001, Aphex Twin largely went quiet, releasing under other aliases instead. Then Syro arrived in 2014 as his first Aphex Twin album in 13 years. It went on to win the Grammy for Best Dance/Electronic Album.

cards.hook: The grinning-face era: a video so unsettling it became a landmark of the form
cards.story: In 1999 Aphex Twin released Windowlicker on Warp, its video directed by Chris Cunningham. The pairing followed their earlier work on Come to Daddy in 1997. Both clips fused James's music with Cunningham's disturbing, digitally-morphed imagery and are widely cited as landmarks of music video.

cards.hook: His pattern held: after a long silence, a sudden 2023 return that earned another Grammy nod
cards.story: Aphex Twin has long alternated long silences with bursts of generosity. In 2023 he released the Blackbox Life Recorder 21f / In a Room7 F760 EP on Warp, led by Blackbox Life Recorder 21f. The track went on to earn a Grammy nomination, extending a career that has stayed unpredictable while remaining beloved.

cards.hook: Shot days before a peace concert, he played anyway — then left Jamaica and made his masterpiece
cards.story: In December 1976 gunmen stormed Bob Marley's Kingston home and wounded him just before the Smile Jamaica concert. He took the stage two days later despite his injuries, then went into self-imposed exile in London. There, in a country far from home, he recorded Exodus — the album that turned him into a global figure.

cards.hook: Time magazine called this record the best album of the entire 20th century
cards.story: Exodus, released in 1977, gathered anthems like 'Jamming', 'One Love/People Get Ready' and the title track into one record made during Marley's London exile. In 1999 Time magazine named it the best album of the 20th century. It remains the door many listeners walk through to reach reggae.

cards.hook: 55,600 fans, one stadium, and the concert that invented the stadium show
cards.story: On August 15, 1965, The Beatles played New York's Shea Stadium in front of roughly 55,600 people, a pop-concert attendance record at the time. The scale was unprecedented — no band had attempted a show this big. The screaming crowds largely drowned out the music, a sign of the touring pressures that would soon push the band off the road.

It was the band's final public performance, captured in the Let It Be film and revisited in Peter Jackson's Get Back.
